patagonia.summary: Industry leader in environmental responsibility and supply chain transparency. Uses recycled materials and repairs products for life.

reformation.summary: Strong focus on sustainable materials, carbon neutrality, and transparent manufacturing. Great for trendy sustainable fashion.

everlane.summary: Built on 'Radical Transparency' regarding costs and factories. Good ethical practices but room for improvement in materials.

levi's.summary: Good water-saving initiatives (Water<Less™ technology), improving transparency, but large scale presents challenges.

nike.summary: Mixed performance. Strong innovation in sustainable materials (Move to Zero), but labor concerns in supply chain persist.

adidas.summary: Good eco-initiatives with ocean plastic shoes. Labor practices improving but still have room for growth.

gucci.summary: Luxury sector leader in going carbon neutral and using sustainable materials. Part of Kering group with strong sustainability focus.

prada.summary: Committed to recycled nylon (Re-Nylon project). Increasing transparency in supply chain.

Stella McCartney.summary: Pioneer in luxury sustainable fashion. No animal products, strong circular economy principles.

zara.summary: Fast fashion model raises concerns about waste and labor conditions. Some sustainability initiatives but volume is high.

h&m.summary: Has sustainability initiatives (Conscious collection, garment collecting), but fast fashion volume undermines efforts.

shein.summary: Ultra-fast fashion with significant environmental and ethical concerns. Low transparency and high waste.

uniqlo.summary: Focus on durability and functionality (LifeWear). Transparency improving but labor practices need more disclosure.

gap.summary: Some sustainability goals but progress slow. Mixed labor records in supply chain.

old navy.summary: Part of Gap Inc. Affordable fast fashion with similar sustainability challenges.

madewell.summary: Good denim recycling program. Improving transparency but still part of larger corporate structure.

allbirds.summary: B Corp certified. Uses natural materials (wool, eucalyptus). Carbon neutral footprint labeling.

veja.summary: Fair trade sneakers. Uses organic cotton and wild rubber. Strong transparency and ethical sourcing.

toms.summary: One for One model. Investing in sustainable materials and ethical manufacturing.

ralph lauren.summary: Some sustainability goals set, but progress slow. Traditional luxury model with room for improvement.

calvin klein.summary: Part of PVH Corp. Some sustainability initiatives but transparency limited.

tommy hilfiger.summary: Also PVH Corp. Similar sustainability profile to Calvin Klein.

the north face.summary: Good recycled materials program. Parent company VF Corp has sustainability goals.

columbia.summary: Some eco-initiatives but transparency could improve.

arcteryx.summary: High-quality durable gear. Increasing use of recycled materials. Limited transparency.

lululemon.summary: Good durability (less waste). Some sustainable material initiatives. Labor practices improving.

puma.summary: Some sustainability programs. Part of Kering group historically, now independent.

asos.summary: Online fast fashion. Some sustainability efforts but volume concerns.

boohoo.summary: Ultra-fast fashion with significant ethical and environmental concerns.

prettylittlething.summary: Part of Boohoo Group. Similar fast fashion concerns.

mango.summary: Some sustainability commitments but transparency limited.

cos.summary: Part of H&M group but better quality/fewer collections. Minimalist aesthetic with some sustainable materials.

& other stories.summary: Also H&M group. Similar sustainability profile to COS.

zara home.summary: Part of Inditex (Zara). Similar sustainability approach.

bershka.summary: Also Inditex. Young fast fashion with similar profile.

pull&bear.summary: Inditex brand. Fast fashion for younger demographic.

massimo dutti.summary: Inditex premium brand. Better quality but similar transparency.

jack & jones.summary: Part of Bestseller. Some initiatives but limited transparency.

vero moda.summary: Also Bestseller. Similar profile to Jack & Jones.

only.summary: Bestseller brand. Fast fashion with limited transparency.

selected.summary: Bestseller's premium brand. Slightly better quality.

gant.summary: Preppy heritage brand. Some sustainability initiatives.

armani.summary: Limited sustainability information. Some fur-free commitments.

versace.summary: Limited sustainability data. Part of Capri Holdings.

burberry.summary: Stopped burning unsold goods. Good transparency, some sustainable materials.

chanel.summary: Limited transparency. Some sustainability commitments but privately held.

dior.summary: Part of LVMH. Limited public sustainability data.

louis vuitton.summary: LVMH group has sustainability goals but limited transparency per brand.

hermes.summary: Focus on craftsmanship and durability (less waste). Limited transparency on labor.

cartier.summary: Part of Richemont. Some responsible sourcing initiatives for gold/diamonds.

tiffany & co.summary: Good responsible sourcing for diamonds and precious metals.

pandora.summary: Committed to using recycled silver and gold. Good transparency.

swarovski.summary: Some sustainability initiatives. Crystal is lead-free since 2012.

ray-ban.summary: Part of EssilorLuxottica. Limited sustainability data.

warby parker.summary: B Corp certified. Good social mission (buy a pair, give a pair).
